prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_audit_entity producer_component_p1)
(register_audit_entity consumer_component_c1)
(register_audit_entity audit_coordinator_alpha)
(register_audit_entity audit_coordinator_beta)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity producer_component_p1 log_channel_a)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity consumer_component_c1 log_channel_b)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha log_channel_c)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(prepare_producer_stream_for_collection producer_component_p1 producer_stream_s1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity producer_component_p1 operator_identity_ops)
(lock_producer_partition_for_composition producer_component_p1 producer_stream_s1 operator_identity_ops)
(release_operator_identity_from_entity producer_component_p1 operator_identity_ops)
(release_event_schema_from_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(prepare_consumer_stream_for_collection consumer_component_c1 consumer_stream_s1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity consumer_component_c1 operator_identity_ops)
(lock_consumer_partition_for_composition consumer_component_c1 consumer_stream_s1 operator_identity_ops)
(release_operator_identity_from_entity consumer_component_c1 operator_identity_ops)
(compose_audit_artifact producer_component_p1 consumer_component_c1 producer_stream_s1 consumer_stream_s1 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(seal_audit_artifact audit_artifact_a1 producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_producer_component producer_component_p1 audit_artifact_a1)
(finalize_consumer_component consumer_component_c1 audit_artifact_a1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_entity producer_component_p1 credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_producer_with_credential producer_component_p1 log_channel_a credential_creds1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_entity consumer_component_c1 credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_consumer_with_credential consumer_component_c1 log_channel_b credential_creds1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(ingest_evidence_fragment_into_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha operator_identity_ops)
(attach_encryption_key_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha encryption_key_k1)
(attach_timestamp_token_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha timestamp_token_t1)
(prepare_coordinator_with_fragment audit_coordinator_alpha evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1 event_schema_v1)
(apply_encryption_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha encryption_key_k1 operator_identity_ops evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1)
(apply_timestamp_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha timestamp_token_t1 evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_operator_identity_from_entity audit_coordinator_alpha operator_identity_ops)
(release_encryption_key_from_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha encryption_key_k1)
(release_timestamp_token_from_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha timestamp_token_t1)
(commit_coordinator_phase1 audit_coordinator_alpha)
(release_event_schema_from_entity audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta log_channel_a)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection audit_coordinator_beta event_schema_v1)
(ingest_evidence_fragment_into_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta operator_identity_ops)
(attach_encryption_key_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta encryption_key_k1)
(attach_timestamp_token_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta timestamp_token_t1)
(prepare_coordinator_with_fragment audit_coordinator_beta evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1 event_schema_v1)
(apply_encryption_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta encryption_key_k1 operator_identity_ops evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1)
(apply_timestamp_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta timestamp_token_t1 evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1)
(commit_coordinator_phase1 audit_coordinator_beta)
(release_event_schema_from_entity audit_coordinator_beta event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_coordinator_with_credential audit_coordinator_alpha log_channel_c credential_creds1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta event_schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_coordinator_with_credential audit_coordinator_beta log_channel_a credential_creds1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many entities and actions. I need to find a plan that achieves the goal: finalizing producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, audit_coordinator_alpha, and audit_coordinator_beta.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves coordinating multiple components: producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, and two audit coordinators, audit_coordinator_alpha and audit_coordinator_beta.  
- The goal is to finalize all these entities, suggesting a need for a structured plan to integrate and align their functions.  
- The complexity arises from managing interactions between these entities, requiring a clear strategy to achieve completion.

## Identifying Available Resources  
- Artifact a1 is shared for both audit coordinators, alpha with fragment f1 and beta with f2.  
- Policies p1, p2, compliance c1, c2, credential creds1, and key k1 are available, supporting entity finalization.  
- External reference xref1 is available and bound to alpha, but xref2, bound to beta, is listed as unavailable, potentially requiring handling.  

## Examining Entity Finalization Steps  
- All entities—producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, audit_coordinator_alpha, and audit_coordinator_beta—must be registered first, as none are initially registered.  
- Each entity needs channel allocation, schema binding, preparation, and credential binding to finalize, with channels reusable upon completion.  
- Producers and consumers require additional steps like stream binding, evidence handling, and artifact composition before sealing and finalization.  

## Analyzing Coordination and Dependencies  
- Artifact a1 composition depends on producer and consumer readiness, involving partition locking and stream preparation.  
- Coordinators need fragments ingested after artifact sealing, suggesting parallel processes for entities with shared resources.  
- Channel allocation might face constraints with only three channels initially available for four entities, potentially requiring sequential processing.

## Examining Entity States and Transitions  
- Composing an artifact with encryption/timestamp requires evidence attached and partition locked, but not ready for composition initially.  
- After composing with request_encryption, the artifact is composed, allowing ingest to set stream_ready and ready_for_comp, releasing evidence locks.  

## Analyzing Preparation Paths  
- The operator path involves preparing streams, binding operators, and locking partitions to achieve ready_for_comp, without evidence locking.  
- The evidence path allows attaching evidence first, then composing with encryption/timestamp, and finally ingesting to reach ready_for_comp, maintaining evidence locks until ingest.  

## Detailing Coordinator Processes  
- Sealing an audit artifact requires it to be composed, producer partition locked, and bound to the producer's schema, then sets it as sealed.  
- Ingesting evidence fragments into coordinators involves ensuring the artifact is sealed, binding fragments, and setting ingested status, enabling further policy actions.

## Planning Entity Registration and Channel Allocation  
- All four entities—producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, audit_coordinator_alpha, and audit_coordinator_beta—must be registered first due to shared resources and limited credentials.  
- Initial channel allocation assigns log_channel_a to p1, log_channel_b to c1, and log_channel_c to alpha, with beta waiting for channel availability after another entity finalizes.  

## Managing Shared Schema Binding  
- Only one event_schema_v1 is available, requiring sequential binding, preparation, and release for each entity to ensure schema availability.  
- The process involves binding schema to an entity, authorizing for collection, and releasing it to bind to the next, starting with producer and consumer due to stream preparation needs.  

## Executing Producer Preparation Steps  
- After registering and allocating a channel, bind event_schema_v1 to producer_component_p1 and authorize for collection, setting it as prepared.  
- Prepare the producer stream for collection, bind the operator identity, and lock the partition, achieving partition_locked, ready_for_comp, and stream_ready states for p1.  

## Handling Consumer and Further Steps  
- Release the schema from p1 to bind it to c1, ensuring c1 is registered and has channel allocation for the process to continue.  
- The same preparation steps—schema binding, authorization, stream preparation, operator binding, and partition locking—will follow for c1, maintaining the sequential resource-sharing approach.

## Preparing Coordinator Alpha  
- Encrypt evidence fragment f1 for audit_coordinator_alpha using encryption_key_k1 and operator_identity_ops, setting enc_applied once conditions like policy_started and bound_key are met.  
- Attach timestamp_token_t1 to alpha, bind it, then apply the timestamp to coordinator, setting timestamp_applied and making alpha eligible for credential binding after committing phase 1.  

## Managing Channel and Credential Availability  
- Release event_schema_v1 from audit_coordinator_alpha to make it available, then bind it to producer_component_p1 for credential attachment.  
- Finalize producer_component_p1 by attaching credential_creds1 and using log_channel_a, freeing both channel and credential, setting p1 as finalized.  

## Continuing Consumer Preparation  
- Release event_schema_v1 from producer_component_p1 to bind it to consumer_component_c1, continuing the sequential schema-sharing process.  
- Prepare to attach credential_creds1 to consumer_component_c1, ensuring it meets eligibility and binding requirements for further steps.

## Continuing Consumer Preparation  
- After finalizing producer_component_p1 and consumer_component_c1, channels a and b are available, with c allocated to audit_coordinator_alpha and a to audit_coordinator_beta.  
- Ensure credential_creds1 remains available for further binding after initial use with p1 and c1.  

## Managing Coordinator Schema Binding  
- Release event_schema_v1 from audit_coordinator_beta to make it available, then rebind it to audit_coordinator_alpha for credential attachment.  
- Finalize audit_coordinator_alpha by attaching credential_creds1 and using log_channel_c, freeing both channel and credential.  

## Finalizing Coordinator Beta  
- Release event_schema_v1 from audit_coordinator_alpha and rebind it to audit_coordinator_beta for credential binding.  
- Finalize audit_coordinator_beta using credential_creds1 and log_channel_a, freeing resources and marking beta as complete.  

## Reviewing Operator and Resource Usage  
- Operator_identity_ops was bound to audit_coordinator_alpha, then released after timestamp application to bind to beta, ensuring proper sequence.  
- Unused resources like evidence_resource_r1, external references, compliance, and policy_template were skipped, aligning with the coordinator path taken.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
